premise: I raised my stepdaughter from age six and drained $140,000 of my nursing salary to put her through college, but after my husband’s cancer diagnosis she secretly forged my signature on his $300,000 life insurance beneficiary form and collected every cent. A stray tax document led me to the forgery a decade later — I hired a forensic examiner, got her convicted of insurance fraud, and when the judgment forced the sale of her house, I bought it at auction for $120,000 and now rent it to her mother, who never believed me.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My Stepdaughter Stole My Husband's $300K Life Insurance And Left Me Penniless. S
